posts.content:
[Predictions]

Charles Radtke vs Francisco Prado
Pick: Radtke via decision
73% of Tapology users (41 users) are picking Radtke (86% decision)
Prado is 11.9 years younger (23.8 vs 35.8)
Radtke has 3” reach edge (72” vs 69”)
Radtke has +0.17 strike differential/min vs -0.86
Radtke proj. +5.4 significant strikes attempted
Radtke proj. +9.0 significant strikes landed
Radtke proj. +18.6 head strikes landed
Radtke proj. 0.63 knockdowns vs 0.41
Prado proj. 2.9 takedowns landed (4.9 attempts) vs 1.8 (4.4 attempts)
Radtke proj. 0.70 submission attempts vs 0.50
Prado proj. 46.9% finish chance vs 25.0% and 28.1% decision
Prado is a good play if he is significant betting favorite

posts.author: Goat******76
posts.content:
[Predictions]

@madaMMA  Couple issues with that:

Aaron is a glass cannon, all gas, no breaks, and has a chin spottier than a Dalmatian. Patricio is not just a counter striker, he’s a bladed stance counter striker, which are extremely rare and Patricio, even in the twilight of his career, still has speed and power in his hands, as well as knowing how to use zig zag footwork to bait boxing exchanges to land his rear hand. If Aaron plans on using his typical gameplan of marching forward and scrap until he sees an opportunity to go for a bodylock or single leg, he’s inevitably going to get clipped, unless he chooses to get Patricio’s back early and try to choke him, which is unlikely since Patricio has very solid grappling defense, and Aaron hasn’t had a submission victory since 2021. And that was against Aiden Lee. Who sucks.

I understand Patricio has looked very unremarkable as of lately in comparison to his prime years, but stylistically Aaron winning this looks very slim IMO.

posts.author: madaMMA
posts.content:
[Predictions]

@Goat******76 you make multiple valid points

But you are overthinking it

Pick pico trust

I also think pico is a way better wrestler and I think he can even stall pitbull against the cage because pitbull has shown zero urgency. And on the feet though pico has bad defense, his offensive boxing is nasty and I think he can overwhelm an old withered pitbull.

posts.author: bornmenu
posts.content:
[Predictions]

The Van disrespect is crazy, and it sucks the Pantoja fight played out the way it did because I honestly believe Van beats Pantoja 7/10 times; but now he's facing someone who is a completely different fighter than Pantoja. Taira is the more technical striker, and won't be as open to counters like Pantoja, who throws flurry punches to set up a double leg, and Taira will have the better judo to get Van to the ground. Van has shown to have good get-ups and TDD, but I believe once it hits the ground, he will struggle massively and possibly get finished...I think it is Taira's time right now, for however long it may be.

posts.author: flavorpacket
posts.content:
[Predictions]

what's the ufc's thought process giving hokit the #5 ranked heavyweight? was there no one else ranked 10 through 15 available? i know heavyweight is **** so they're trying to push hokit but this seems like an enormous step up in comp. blaydes has only been KO'd by monsters. and blaydes is basically in a different league compared to freeman and gimenis so it's not like hokit has a ton of relevant experience to help him out here. blaydes isn't the most dangerous heavyweight. but right now i'm thinking as long as blaydes survives r1 he should cruise. it's a lot harder for me to see hokit knocking out blaydes in a round, even though that's probably what the ufc wants, i'm having a very hard time seeing how the ufc gets what they want here.

i guess it could also be one of those character building fights where hokit is forced to go 3 hard rounds and loses a 29-28 and blaydes protects the gate. and they'll probably put hokit in the top 15 afterwards anyway. that seems like a possible outcome since blaydes just had a similar fight against kuniev.

right now i'm thinking blaydes by decision or hokit r1 tko if the stars align and the ufc gets what they want.

* Edited at 04.01.2026,  9:47 PM ET *

posts.author: lettmma
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Van vs. Taira
Tatsuro Taira, SUB, R2
Taira Submission. Van's run at 125 has been crazy. His fight with Royval was a breakout performance and one of the best scraps I have seen. But I think Taira has the facilities to stop Van here. Watching the Tsuruya fight back, he struggled to manipulate Van's balance whenever they would clinch. Tsuruya did not have the facilities to take Van's back, off balance, and secure a dominant position. Taira has this ability in his game. Taira dominated Royval and Moreno in the grappling. Taira did leave his head on the centerline in the Royval fight, but head movement is a hole Taira can add and learn quickly. Van will have to develop a complete cage wrestling system. He will have to account for back take defense against a body lock, submission defense, avoiding being flatten out and trips against the cage. If Taira gets his back, I think the fight will be over shortly.

posts.author: Spockling
posts.content:

posts.author: lettmma
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Procházka vs. Ulberg
Jiří Procházka, TKO, R3
Jiri KO. Jiri's fights are the most fascinating thing to watch. Carlos is a really great technical striker. But I think Jiri will implement his game no matter what. I do not think Ulberg will be able to keep his wits in the chaos. Jiri has a controlled chaos/recklessness. He isn't going to be reckless like Meinfeild because Jiri has great finishing instincts and knows how to time his finishing sequences. Jiri will get hurt this fight but he is more durable than Reyes, in hindsight people knew Reyes run wouldn't last. Jiri isn't going to be scared to enter the pocket and take one to give one. His head movement has improved and he rolled well with punches from Rountree and Hill. Once Jiri lands a good shot on in Round 2 or 3 I think we will see a huge momentum shift and a spectacularly dramatic finish by Jiri.

* Edited at 04.02.2026, 12:28 PM ET *
